author: adeliedev
text: @jtrevorchapman i love when bots have web dashboards its beautiful but i just get lazy and stick with terminals

author: jtrevorchapman
text: @adeliedev I stick with terminal during the buildout, too. Final piece is the dashboard when I'm ready to let it cook for multiple days... easier to just scan the dashboard than look at the tui.

author: radcap_
text: @jtrevorchapman building out something similar in a paper trading environment for a couple weeks now -- with so much friction built in what is it exactly about live that makes it even more difficult?

author: jtrevorchapman
text: @radcap_ Really the paper trading is just to verify no bugs in the logic. Results from paper never translate to live trading. This model failed. Volatile regimes (3 or more reversals) it did excellent. Refined with a clear winner from tick one it failed miserable preferring cheap side.

author: Pxlern
text: @jtrevorchapman Good stuff but need to get that pair cost down 😅

author: jtrevorchapman
text: @Pxlern With hundreds of pairs across all different market regimes, targeting roughly .985 is the lowest I can go to stay profitable in all market types.

author: MarcusAkvilla
text: @jtrevorchapman please tell me, how do you get the prices and the glass for the event in the code? For some reason, my bot doesn't want to load them. And there is no stable operation of the websocket.

author: adeliedev
text: @jtrevorchapman polymarket lately really hammered by bots so fills are terrible!! i think they need to up their game by upgrade their infra to use rust (no rage bait intended)

author: palladinojam
text: @jtrevorchapman Definitely wanna know how it went for ya bro!!
